title: Generate SLSA provenance attestation for build artefacts
current: CI runs typecheck, lint, test, build, audit, and secret scan. No SBOM is generated and no SLSA provenance attestation is produced for the build output.
proposed: Add a new step to the `verify` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` after the Build step: `uses: actions/attest-build-provenance@v2` (pin to SHA). Also add `id-token: write` and `attestations: write` to that job's permissions block. This produces a signed SLSA provenance attestation attached to the workflow run, satisfying SLSA L2 for the build artefact.
reason: The CI workflow pins actions to full commit SHAs (e.g. actions/checkout@9c091bb…) which satisfies SLSA L1 for action integrity, but the template generates no SBOM and no SLSA provenance attestation for its own build artefacts. SLSA L2 requires a hosted build service that generates provenance; the Gi

title: Add CycloneDX SBOM generation to CI
current: No SBOM is generated in CI or as part of the build process. `npm audit` checks for known CVEs but produces no exportable dependency inventory.
proposed: Add a `sbom` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that runs `npx @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm --output-format JSON --output-file sbom.json` and uploads `sbom.json` as a workflow artefact via `actions/upload-artifact`. Pin the npx invocation to a specific version (e.g. `@cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm@1.19.3`). Document the SBOM artefact location in `40-tooling-supply-chain.mdc`.
source: CycloneDX for Node.js — @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm (github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-node-npm); NIST SP 800-204D; US EO 14028
reason: The template has no SBOM generation step. CycloneDX and SPDX are the two OWASP/NIST-recognised SBOM formats; the `@cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm` CLI produces a CycloneDX SBOM from `package-lock.json` in one command and is the official CycloneDX tool for npm projects. Without an SBOM, there is no machine-

title: Enforce npm provenance checks and exact pinning for production dependencies
current: `40-tooling-supply-chain.mdc` requires `npm ci` in CI and `npm audit` at high severity, but does not require checking npm provenance attestations when adding new packages, and does not mention `npm install --save-exact` or a policy on range width.
proposed: In `40-tooling-supply-chain.mdc`, add two rules: (1) When adding a new package, run `npm install --save-exact` for production dependencies to record the exact resolved version; use ranges only for devDependencies where drift risk is lower. (2) For new packages published after mid-2023, verify provenance with `npm audit signatures` and check the provenance attestation on npmjs.com before adding — flag any package without provenance in the plan. reason: All `package.json` dependencies use SemVer ranges (`^`), meaning `npm install` on a fresh clone can resolve to a different dependency tree than the one tested in CI if a minor or patch release introduces a regression or a supply-chain compromise. The `package-lock.json` mitigates this for direct ins

title: Pin CI container images to digest (SHA256) not just version tag
current: CI pulls `semgrep/semgrep:1.168.0` and `ghcr.io/gitleaks/gitleaks:v8.30.1` by version tag. No digest pin is present.
proposed: In `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, replace the tag-only references with digest-pinned references, e.g. `ghcr.io/gitleaks/gitleaks:v8.30.1@sha256:<digest>` and `semgrep/semgrep:1.168.0@sha256:<digest>`. Add a comment with the tag for human readability (same pattern as the action SHA pins already in the file). Document in `40-tooling-supply-chain.mdc` that container images in CI must be pinned by digest, not tag.
source: SLSA Provenance L2 — mutable tags as a supply-chain risk (slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/threats); Docker content trust (docs.docker.com/engine/security/trust/)
reason: The `sast` job uses `semgrep/semgrep:1.168.0` (a specific version tag) and the `gitleaks` job uses `ghcr.io/gitleaks/gitleaks:v8.30.1` (a specific version tag).
